title: Relative Velocity Combat
summary: Combat effectiveness depends on relative, not absolute, velocity.
details: Two ships at 10 km/s on parallel courses have zero relative velocity - they're stationary to each other. Two ships at 100 m/s on collision course have 200 m/s relative velocity. High relative velocity means brief engagement windows but difficult targeting. Low relative velocity allows sustained engagement but predictable positioning. Control relative velocity to control engagement.
annotations: Matching velocity creates stable firing platforms.
